mission.title: Install and Configure Crossplane on Kubernetes
mission.description: Crossplane is a framework for building cloud native control planes without needing to write code. It allows you to orchestrate applications and infrastructure across various environments, making it a powerful tool for managing cloud resources.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Crossplane Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add crossplane-stable https://charts.crossplane.io/stable
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Crossplane
steps.description:
Install Crossplane using Helm, creating a namespace for it.
```bash
helm install crossplane crossplane-stable/crossplane --namespace crossplane-system --create-namespace --version 1.17.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Crossplane pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane-system
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access Crossplane API
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Crossplane API service for easy access.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/crossplane --namespace crossplane-system 8080:443
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall Crossplane from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall crossplane --namespace crossplane-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Crossplane resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) related to Crossplane.
```bash
kubectl delete crd `kubectl get crd | grep crossplane.io | awk '{print $1}'`
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Crossplane namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Crossplane namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace crossplane-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current state of Crossplane resources before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane-system -o yaml > crossplane-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Crossplane
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Crossplane to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade crossplane crossplane-stable/crossplane --namespace crossplane-system --version 1.17.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Crossplane pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see pods in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more information.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n crossplane-system
``` 
Ensure that the configuration is correct and that all dependencies are met.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm install fails with a 'chart not found' error, verify that the correct Helm repository is added and updated.
```bash
helm repo list
helm repo update
```

troubleshooting.title: Crossplane API not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Crossplane API, ensure that the port-forward command is running and that the service is correctly configured.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n crossplane-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Crossplane will have all pods running in the 'crossplane-system' namespace without errors. You should be able to access the Crossplane API via the configured port-forward.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add crossplane-stable https://charts.crossplane.io/stable
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl on your local machine.
